nodes.en: Patents are legal documents recording inventions, their novel technical features, functional principles, and enforceable claims. They balance knowledge disclosure against temporary monopoly rights for inventors.
nodes.cs: Patenty jsou právní dokumenty zaznamenávající vynálezy, jejich inovativní technické prvky, funkční principy a vymáhatelné nároky. Vyvažují zveřejnění vědomostí proti dočasným exkluzivním právům pro vynálezce.
nodes.brief:
A patent is a legal instrument that grants an inventor a time-limited monopoly over a technical invention in exchange for full public disclosure of how it works — its novel features, functional principles, and the enforceable claims that define the scope of protection. This makes patents a hybrid: simultaneously a legal contract with the state and a technical description detailed enough that a skilled practitioner could reproduce the invention once the exclusivity period expires.

The technical description inside a patent frequently takes the same graphical form as [[09.08]] Blueprints & Schematics, since drawings are the clearest way to disclose mechanical or structural novelty; unlike a blueprint used for fabrication, though, a patent drawing exists to establish legal priority and claim scope. Patents also interact with [[09.05]] Standards & Specifications when a patented technology becomes essential to an industry standard, creating the standard-essential-patent licensing regime. Taken cumulatively across decades, patent filings form a documentary record of technical progress that overlaps with [[09.10]] Historical Archives, and the underlying inventions belong to the same [[03]] Applied Sciences & Technology domain the disclosures describe.

Patent search and full-text disclosure are freely accessible through national and international offices: the [World Intellectual Property Organization](https://www.wipo.int/portal/en/index.html) provides global patent search (PATENTSCOPE), and the [United States Patent and Trademark Office](https://www.uspto.gov/) maintains the full historical US patent record.
